title: Base64 Encoder/Decoder: Encode & Decode Text and Files
description: Did you know 33% of data overhead comes from this one encoding trick? Convert text, URLs, and images to Base64 instantly before you deploy your next API.

mainEntity.name: How to base64 encode a file?

acceptedAnswer.text: To Base64 encode a file, you can use our online Base64 encoder tool. Select the 'File Upload' option, choose your image or document, and the tool will instantly convert the file into a Base64 data URI that you can copy and use directly in HTML or CSS.

mainEntity.name: How to base64 decode a string?

acceptedAnswer.text: To Base64 decode a string, paste your Base64 encoded text into the decoder panel of our tool. It will automatically detect the format and translate the encoded characters back into readable plain text.

No, Base64 is not an encryption method. It is an encoding scheme used to represent binary data in an ASCII string format. It provides no security or confidentiality and can be easily decoded by anyone using a Base64 converter.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between Base64 and Base64URL?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard Base64 uses the characters '+' and '/' which can conflict with URL formatting. Base64URL replaces '+' with '-' and '/' with '_', making the resulting encoded string safe to be included in URLs and filenames without requiring further URL encoding.

mainEntity.name: Does Base64 encoding increase file size?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, Base64 encoding increases the original file or text size by approximately 33%. This is because it uses 4 ASCII characters to represent every 3 bytes of binary data. Therefore, it is best used for small assets like icons or short tokens.

schemas.headline: Base64 Encoder/Decoder: Encode & Decode Text and Files Online
schemas.description: Convert text, URLs, and images to Base64 instantly. Learn what Base64 is and how to use it safely in web development.
